title: Highly Cited Researchers
description: You are given three tables: researchers, papers they authored, and a citations table recording which papers cite which other papers. Return names of researchers whose papers have been cited more than 3 times in total. Order alphabetically.
inputFormat:
researchers(id, name, institution)
papers(id, title, researcher_id, year)
citations(id, cited_paper_id, citing_paper_id)
outputFormat: One researcher name per line, ordered alphabetically.
constraints: Use JOIN and GROUP BY. Count rows in the citations table where the cited_paper_id belongs to the researcher. Use HAVING COUNT(*) > 3. Include ORDER BY name. Only SELECT queries are allowed.
sampleSolution: SELECT r.name FROM researchers r JOIN papers p ON r.id = p.researcher_id JOIN citations c ON p.id = c.cited_paper_id GROUP BY r.id, r.name HAVING COUNT(*) > 3 ORDER BY r.name;
hint: Join researchers → papers → citations (on cited_paper_id), then GROUP BY researcher with HAVING COUNT(*) > 3.

testCases.setupSql: CREATE TABLE researchers (id INTEGER, name TEXT, institution TEXT); CREATE TABLE papers (id INTEGER, title TEXT, researcher_id INTEGER, year INTEGER); CREATE TABLE citations (id INTEGER, cited_paper_id INTEGER, citing_paper_id INTEGER); INSERT INTO researchers VALUES (1,'Alice','MIT'),(2,'Bob','Stanford'),(3,'Charlie','Harvard'); INSERT INTO papers VALUES (1,'Paper A',1,2020),(2,'Paper B',1,2021),(3,'Paper C',2,2022),(4,'Paper D',3,2023); INSERT INTO citations VALUES (1,1,3),(2,1,4),(3,2,3),(4,2,4),(5,3,1),(6,4,1);

CREATE TABLE researchers (id INTEGER, name TEXT, institution TEXT); CREATE TABLE papers (id INTEGER, title TEXT, researcher_id INTEGER, year INTEGER); CREATE TABLE citations (id INTEGER, cited_paper_id INTEGER, citing_paper_id INTEGER); INSERT INTO researchers VALUES (1,'Diana','Oxford'),(2,'Eve','Cambridge'),(3,'Frank','ETH'); INSERT INTO papers VALUES (1,'Study X',1,2019),(2,'Study Y',2,2020),(3,'Study Z',2,2021),(4,'Report W',3,2022); INSERT INTO citations VALUES (1,1,2),(2,1,3),(3,1,4),(4,2,1),(5,2,4),(6,3,1),(7,3,4),(8,4,2);

CREATE TABLE researchers (id INTEGER, name TEXT, institution TEXT); CREATE TABLE papers (id INTEGER, title TEXT, researcher_id INTEGER, year INTEGER); CREATE TABLE citations (id INTEGER, cited_paper_id INTEGER, citing_paper_id INTEGER); INSERT INTO researchers VALUES (1,'Grace','Yale'),(2,'Hank','Princeton'); INSERT INTO papers VALUES (1,'Paper G',1,2021),(2,'Paper H',2,2022); INSERT INTO citations VALUES (1,1,2),(2,2,1);

CREATE TABLE researchers (id INTEGER, name TEXT, institution TEXT); CREATE TABLE papers (id INTEGER, title TEXT, researcher_id INTEGER, year INTEGER); CREATE TABLE citations (id INTEGER, cited_paper_id INTEGER, citing_paper_id INTEGER); INSERT INTO researchers VALUES (1,'Iris','Caltech'),(2,'Jack','Columbia'),(3,'Kate','NYU'); INSERT INTO papers VALUES (1,'Paper I',1,2018),(2,'Paper J',2,2019),(3,'Paper K',3,2020); INSERT INTO citations VALUES (1,1,2),(2,1,3),(3,1,2),(4,1,3),(5,2,1),(6,2,3),(7,2,1),(8,2,3),(9,3,1),(10,3,2),(11,3,1),(12,3,2);

CREATE TABLE researchers (id INTEGER, name TEXT, institution TEXT); CREATE TABLE papers (id INTEGER, title TEXT, researcher_id INTEGER, year INTEGER); CREATE TABLE citations (id INTEGER, cited_paper_id INTEGER, citing_paper_id INTEGER); INSERT INTO researchers VALUES (1,'Leo','UCL'),(2,'Mia','KCL'); INSERT INTO papers VALUES (1,'Paper L',1,2020),(2,'Paper M',1,2021),(3,'Paper N',2,2022); INSERT INTO citations VALUES (1,1,3),(2,2,3),(3,1,3),(4,2,3),(5,1,3);
